notes: Cathedral gravity deck · medium-mode (WATCH PARTY). The room is louder than usual — overlapping conversations, the show streaming on the cassette deck, the critic's commentary, social tension that sharpens as the hour goes on. 16 cards. The arty-farty / nerdy-reference flavor is the room itself; the cards press DOUBT (a host's enemy) more than STAGNATION.

cards.title: THE SHOW IS STARTING
cards.flavor: Elicia's voice through the cassette deck — recorded, but live with you. Everyone shushes. The room narrows to the speakers.

effects.text: everyone is listening to her now.

cards.title: A REFERENCE LANDS
cards.flavor: Elicia drops a Borges line over the cassette. Three people in the room laugh at the exact same beat. Frasier doesn't.

cards.title: A REFERENCE MISSES
cards.flavor: She drops one only two people catch. Three other people pretend they caught it. The room sags a half-step.

cards.title: THE CRITIC TAKES A NOTE
cards.flavor: His thin notebook is open. He's writing without looking at the page.

cards.title: THE SUPERFAN RECITES
cards.flavor: She knows what comes next in the show. She mouths the line a half-second before Elicia says it. Nobody knows whether to be charmed or alarmed.

effects.note: the room is primed; SHOW is easier

cards.title: THE COLLEGE FRIEND MONOLOGUES
cards.flavor: He has a whole anecdote queued up. He's been queueing it for two hours. He delivers it now, over the show.

cards.title: SOMEONE COMPLIMENTS A PIECE
cards.flavor: Mackenzie, quiet, looking at the EMPRESS letter wall: "this one is good." She doesn't elaborate.

cards.title: SOMEONE BREAKS A GLASS
cards.flavor: Bay-door side. Quick laugh, then a quick apology. Nobody hurt. Glass on the floor for the rest of the night.

cards.title: THE CRITIC ASKS ABOUT THE STEAMBOAT
cards.flavor: He's at the workbench. "What's this one?" he says. He already knows. He wants to hear you say it.

cards.title: THE DEMON ORDERS AGAIN
cards.flavor: He raises two fingers without looking up. The same drink. He has not touched the last one.

cards.title: ELICIA SIGNS OFF
cards.flavor: The show ends. The cassette deck clicks. The room is quieter than it has been all night. Three or four people clap once.

cards.title: THE CRITIC PUBLISHES BEFORE HE LEAVES
cards.flavor: He's on his phone at the bay door. His notes are already a post. Hot take, time-stamped. Frasier doesn't read it.

cards.title: THE DINER MODEL FLICKERS
cards.flavor: At FOOL station, a waiter-figurine has moved one booth over. John is in the cathedral; the figurine of him is in the diner.

cards.title: ELICIA LEFT A TAPE
cards.flavor: A cassette on top of the PRIESTESS pile that wasn't there an hour ago. No note.

cards.title: A DRAFT BURNED ITSELF
cards.flavor: You hadn't lit any match. The corner is curled and gone.

cards.title: THE WIRES UNHOOKED
cards.flavor: A finished arcana installation gone dark. Still finished. Just not alive.
